objective: Repair the HLD Pro self-learning loop by fixing three structural gaps: (1) remove the missing count_since Supabase call from consolidate-memory.sh and replace with local file-based logic, (2) add AIS_SUPABASE_URL and AIS_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to the governance bootstrap target, and (3) add memory_integrity.py to closeout-hook.sh so CI SKIP never silences integrity failures at local closeout.
scope_boundary: Rewrite the count_since block in scripts/consolidate-memory.sh to use local file state only — no Supabase GET call.
Add AIS_SUPABASE_URL and AIS_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to the governance target block in scripts/bootstrap-repo-env.sh.
Add a DRY_RUN=1 test confirming both vars appear in the governance target output.
Add memory_integrity.py invocation (without --allow-missing) to hooks/closeout-hook.sh after the consolidate-memory step, scoped to governance repo.
Update docs/runbooks/always-on-governance.md with AIS credential prerequisite and memory integrity check documentation.
Produce planning artifacts: PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope, OVERLORD_BACKLOG row.
out_of_scope: Implementing count_since or any GET handler in the AIS memory-writer edge function (ai-integration-services repo).
Removing or changing the --allow-missing flag on the CI overlord-sweep.yml step — the runner constraint is unresolvable without self-hosted runners.
Writing implementation code in this planning session.
Modifying the AIS memory-writer write path or schema.
research_summary: Audit on 2026-04-24 identified three structural gaps preventing the operator_context to MEMORY.md write-back loop from executing. Gap 1: scripts/consolidate-memory.sh line 137 calls the AIS memory-writer with action=count_since but the edge function only accepts POST writes — no GET handler exists — so every closeout-hook invocation receives a 405 and skips. Gap 2: the governance bootstrap target in scripts/bootstrap-repo-env.sh does not emit AIS_SUPABASE_URL or AIS_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, so consolidate-memory.sh exits at the credential guard before the API call. Gap 3: overlord-sweep.yml runs memory_integrity.py --allow-missing on ubuntu-latest where the project memory directory does not exist, so all 6 repos SKIP. The working reference for local file-based consolidation is scripts/orchestrator/self_learning.py which uses Path-based traversal with no Supabase calls.

sprints.name: Sprint 1 — Fix count_since: local-only consolidation
sprints.goal: Remove the Supabase count_since HTTP call from consolidate-memory.sh and replace with local file-based counting matching the self_learning.py pattern, so the consolidate step runs without network dependencies or missing-endpoint failures.
tasks: Remove the AIS credential guard block from scripts/consolidate-memory.sh — no Supabase read credentials are needed for the local-only path.
Remove the QUERY_URL construction and curl call that called count_since.
Add local file-based logic to count recent closeout artifacts under raw/closeouts/ since LAST_UPDATE_TS, mirroring the Path-based traversal in scripts/orchestrator/self_learning.py.
Ensure the script remains fail-open: if local file counting fails, log a warning and continue rather than exiting.
Verify the script exits 0 in --dry-run mode without any AIS credentials set.
acceptance_criteria: bash scripts/consolidate-memory.sh --repo hldpro-governance --dry-run exits 0 with no AIS credentials in the environment.
No HTTP request to AIS_SUPABASE_URL is made during consolidate-memory execution.
The MEMORY.md recent-learnings line is updated based on local raw/closeouts/ file count.
The script remains idempotent on repeated invocation.

sprints.name: Sprint 2 — Wire AIS credentials into governance bootstrap target
sprints.goal: Ensure AIS_SUPABASE_URL and AIS_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are emitted by the governance bootstrap target so any future consolidate step that writes to Supabase has credentials available without manual env var injection.
tasks: Add AIS_SUPABASE_URL and AIS_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to the governance target write_env block in scripts/bootstrap-repo-env.sh after the SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN line.
Add a DRY_RUN=1 smoke test confirming both var names appear in the emitted output.
Update docs/runbooks/always-on-governance.md to document the AIS credential prerequisite.
acceptance_criteria: DRY_RUN=1 bash scripts/bootstrap-repo-env.sh governance output contains both AIS_SUPABASE_URL and AIS_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY.
No secrets appear in the DRY_RUN=1 output — only var names or placeholder indicators.

sprints.name: Sprint 3 — Add memory_integrity.py to closeout-hook for real local validation
sprints.goal: Provide real MEMORY.md integrity validation at every local closeout by invoking memory_integrity.py without --allow-missing from hooks/closeout-hook.sh, so CI SKIP never silences integrity failures caught on the operator machine.
tasks: Add a memory_integrity.py invocation after the consolidate-memory step in hooks/closeout-hook.sh, scoped to the governance repo slug.
Run without --allow-missing so a missing or malformed MEMORY.md produces a loud warning.
Make the failure non-fatal (warn and continue) to match the existing consolidate-memory failure mode.
Confirm the overlord-sweep.yml --allow-missing flag remains unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: hooks/closeout-hook.sh invokes python3 scripts/overlord/memory_integrity.py after consolidate-memory.
When MEMORY.md is present and valid, the invocation exits 0 and the hook continues.
When MEMORY.md is absent or malformed, the hook logs a clear warning but does not exit non-zero.

specialist_reviews.focus: Confirm the local-only fix approach for count_since is sound, that no cross-repo edge-function changes are introduced, and that the closeout-hook memory integrity invocation does not break existing fail-open semantics.
specialist_reviews.summary: Review not yet requested. Must be completed before implementation proceeds.

alternate_model_review.summary: Alternate-family review required before implementation proceeds. Reviewer must confirm the local-only count_since fix is safe, the governance bootstrap credential addition does not introduce secret-exposure risk, and the closeout-hook integrity invocation is correctly scoped and fail-open.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Tier 1 planning session only. No implementation code written. Three artifacts produced: PDCAR, structured plan, execution scope. OVERLORD_BACKLOG row added. No edits to scripts/consolidate-memory.sh, scripts/bootstrap-repo-env.sh, hooks/closeout-hook.sh, or any AIS edge function.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Operator reviews and approves artifacts, then opens an implementation session on branch issue-570-self-learning-loop-repair-20260424. Implementation agent reads this plan, obtains alternate-family and specialist reviews, implements three sprints in order, runs all acceptance-criteria checks, and opens a PR.
blocked_on: Operator approval of this planning artifact.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: Operator has reviewed and approved the three-sprint plan.
All three acceptance-criteria checklists pass before PR is opened.

material_deviation_rules: Do NOT implement a count_since GET handler or any read endpoint in the AIS memory-writer edge function. The local-only approach using raw/closeouts/ file state is mandated. Any deviation requires operator approval and a new issue.
Do NOT change the --allow-missing flag or remove skip behavior in .github/workflows/overlord-sweep.yml — the CI runner constraint is unresolvable without self-hosted runners.
Do NOT make the closeout-hook memory_integrity.py invocation fail-closed. The hook must remain fail-open to match the existing consolidate-memory failure mode.
Do NOT write to any file outside the three sprint file_paths lists without opening a new issue.
